name: Vadiraja Tirtha
dates_notes: The 1480-1600 range is the common Dvaita-tradition chronology but implies a very long lifespan, so the entry flags the dates as contested.

lineage_polemical.note: The Yuktimallikā and Nyāyaratnāvalī argue from a Tattva-vāda standpoint against Advaita positions.

core_thesis: Vādirāja Tīrtha is a sixteenth-century Tattva-vāda author and pontiff associated with the Sode maṭha. His philosophical writing continues the Madhva-Jayatīrtha-Vyāsatīrtha line: Viṣṇu is the independent reality, the world and selves are real dependents, and liberation does not erase difference. The Yuktimallikā presents Tattva-vāda through a sequence of reasoned arguments against rival Vedānta positions, while the Nyāyaratnāvalī gives a shorter polemical treatment. His Tīrthaprabandha belongs to the pilgrimage and devotional register rather than formal metaphysics, but it documents the same Vaiṣṇava orientation in a different genre.

engaged_works.title: Yuktimallika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely ascribed to Vādirāja Tīrtha in the Dvaita tradition and modern catalogues.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit Tattva-vāda treatise organized into topical sections that defend the Madhva school's realist Vaiṣṇava metaphysics against rival Vedānta positions. The work is associated with the standard Tattva-vāda claims of Viṣṇu's independence, the reality of the world, the reality of difference, and the rejection of Advaitic world-illusion. The Internet Archive OCR sample checked in this pass was heavily corrupted and was not suitable for ingestion as a clean source file.

engaged_works.title: Nyayaratnavali
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely ascribed in lists of Vādirāja's scholastic works.
engaged_works.summary: A concise Tattva-vāda polemical work, traditionally grouped with Vādirāja's arguments against Advaita. It is relevant to the timeline as evidence of the continued late-medieval Dvaita practice of presenting Madhva's realism in compact argumentative form.

engaged_works.title: Tirthaprabandha
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit pilgrimage work describing sacred places from a Vaiṣṇava perspective. It is not a technical Vedānta treatise, but it records the devotional geography and ritual imagination of the same Tattva-vāda milieu in which Vādirāja's polemical works stand.
